name: go-string-template
description: A standard-library-only string template engine. Render(tmpl string, vars map[string]string) (string, error) expands {{key}} placeholders (whitespace inside the braces is ignored), returning a *PlaceholderError wrapping ErrUnclosed for an unterminated placeholder or ErrUnknownKey for a key absent from vars. A generic render core powers the public helpers. Verified build/vet/test GREEN and gofmt-clean with go 1.23.

maintain.request: Support non-string variable values: add a generic RenderAny[V any](tmpl string, vars map[string]V) (string, error) that formats each substituted value with fmt.Sprint (so ints, bools, fmt.Stringer, etc. work directly) by reusing the existing generic render core, preserving the same unclosed/unknown-key error semantics. Add table-driven tests covering int maps, mixed map[string]any, and both error paths.

review.bug: In the render loop the scan index is advanced by a hardcoded 1 (i = body + end + 1) instead of len(closeDelim) which is 2, so only one of the two closing brace characters is consumed and the leftover '}' is emitted as literal text after every placeholder.
keywords: advances index by one instead of two
